description_long: This is a pair of metal pliers with a dark, weathered finish, showing signs of oxidation and wear, suggesting frequent use or age. The handles are long, slightly curved, and taper towards the ends, providing leverage for gripping and cutting tasks. The jaws are robust and feature a combination of flat gripping surfaces and a central notch, likely for wire cutting or gripping round objects. The pivot joint is a classic fixed type, allowing the two arms to rotate around a single axis for opening and closing the jaws. The surface of the handles appears to be uninsulated and lacks any ergonomic padding, indicating a utilitarian design. No visible branding or logos are present on the tool, emphasizing its functional, no-frills construction.
description: A weathered, dark metal pair of pliers with curved handles and robust jaws featuring a central notch. The utilitarian design shows signs of heavy use and lacks insulation or branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the pliers, curved handles, and the detailed jaw design with a central notch.
description_view_1: Side profile view displaying the thinness of the pliers and the alignment of the handles and jaws.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, highlighting the symmetry and the same jaw and handle features.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the slim profile and the straight alignment of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Long, slightly curved, uninsulated metal handles with a utilitarian shape.
attribute_jaw_design: Robust jaws with flat gripping surfaces and a central notch, suitable for gripping and wire cutting.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single-axis pivot joint, non-adjustable, classic pliers mechanism.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with visible wear, lacking any ribbed or ergonomic grip pattern.
